British Academy Film Awards|75th]] | next = [[77th British Academy Film Awards|77th]] }} The '''76th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[British Academy Film Awards|BAFTAs]], were held on 19 February 2023, honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2022 in film|2022]], at the [[Royal Festival Hall]] within [[London]]'s [[Southbank Centre]].{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/bafta-awards-2023-date-february-1235161286/ |title=BAFTA Sets 2023 Film Awards Date, Bringing Ceremony Back to Traditional February Slot |url=https://deadline.com/2022/09/bafta-2023-film-awards-royal-festival-hall-1235123317/ |title=BAFTA to Move 2023 Film Awards to Southbank Centre's Royal Festival Hall |last=Ntim |first=Zac Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2022. The nominations were announced on 19 January 2023 via a global [[Livestreaming|livestream]], hosted by actors [[Hayley Atwell]] and [[Toheeb Jimoh]], from the arts charity's newly redeveloped HQ at film|epic]] [[List of anti-war films|anti-war]] drama ''[[All Quiet on the Western Front (2022 film)|All Quiet on the Western Front]]'' received the most nominations with fourteen, tying the record set by ''[[Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon]]'' (2000) as the most nominated non-[[English language]] film in the awards' history; winning seven of these, including [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] ([[Edward Berger]]) and [[BAFTA Award for Best Film Not in the English Language|Best Film Not in the English Language]], the [[World War I]] epic set a new record for the most BAFTA wins for a non-English language film.{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-64510093 |title=Baftas 2023: 'All Quiet on the Western Front' by [[Eswatini|Swazi]]-English BAFTA- and [[Academy Awards|Academy Award]]-nominated actor [[Richard E. Academy of Film and Television Arts announces recipients of 2023 Film Awards}} The format of this awards ceremony was altered somewhat by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], with the inclusion of a [[Live television|live broadcast]] of the awards for the final four categories rather than the pre-recorded format used in previous years. There was also a backstage studio for interviews with winners, nominees and others, and various music performances during the broadcast. The broadcast was streamed live by [[BritBox]] in eight other countries: Australia, Canada, Denmark, Finland, Norway, South Africa, Sweden, and the United States. Across all categories, German war film ''[[All Quiet on the Western Front (2022 film)|All Quiet on the Western Front]]'' achieved the most nominations, with fourteen; it was not the only foreign language film to receive major nominations, with this suggested to be reflective of a similar change in consumption of non-English media.{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2023/01/bafta-ceo-on-an-extraordinary-nominations-outcome-spielberg-diversity-1235227086/ |title=BAFTA Heads Talk "Extraordinary" Nominations, Spielberg & Tease "More this was heavily edited out of the television broadcast.{{cite web |title=BAFTAs Accidentally Announce Carey Mulligan as Supporting Actress Winner Instead of Kerry Condon |url=https://variety.com/2023/film/global/carey-mulligan-kerry-condon-best-supporting-actress-baftas-mistake-1235528994/ |last=Yossman |first=K.J. |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=19 February 2023 |access-date=21 February 2023 |archive-date=20 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230220220924/https://variety.com/2023/film/global/carey-mulligan-kerry-condon-best-supporting-actress-baftas-mistake-1235528994/ |url-status=live}} The broadcast concluded with live coverage of four categories (in the following order): [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|Rising Star]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]], and [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]]. The ceremony ran from 7:00 p.m to 9:00 p.m. with a switch from pre-recorded to live broadcast at around 8:30 p.m. ''All Quiet on the Western Front'' won Best Film, as well as taking the most awards, with seven; "We've been blessed with so many nominations and winning this is just incredible," said producer [[Malte Grunert]] while accepting the award on stage. ''The Banshees of Inisherin'' and ''[[Elvis (2022 film)|Elvis]]'' followed with four awards each, but eventually lost all of their nominations at the [[95th Academy Awards]]. ''Everything Everywhere All at Once'', the eventual dominant winner at the [[Academy Awards]], only won one award: [[BAFTA Award for Best Editing|Best Editing]].{{cite web |last1=Shoard |first1=Catherine |last2=Khomami |first2=Nadia |date=19 February 2023 |title=All Quiet on the Western Front sweeps Baftas as Banshees also gets an Oscar boost |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2023/feb/19/all-quiet-on-the-western-front-sweeps-baftas-as-banshees-also-gets-an-oscar-boost |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230219220651/https://www.theguardian.com/film/2023/feb/19/all-quiet-on-the-western-front-sweeps-baftas-as-banshees-also-gets-an-oscar-boost |archive-date=19 February 2023 |access-date=19 February 2023 |work=[[The Guardian]] |url-access=limited}}{{cite web |last1=Harrison |first1=Ellie |last2=Sachdeva |first2=Maanya |date=19 February 2023 |title=Bafta Awards 2023: The biggest talking points, from the top winners to the mistake that was cut from the edit |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/baftas-2023-richard-e-grant-carey-mulligan-b2285440.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230220000007/https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/baftas-2023-richard-e-grant-carey-mulligan-b2285440.html |archive-date=20 February 2023 |access-date=19 February 2023 |work=[[The Independent]]}}{{cite web |url=https://www.cnn.com/2023/02/19/entertainment/bafta-awards-2023-winners-list/index.html |title=BAFTA Awards 2023: See the full list of winners |last=Heching |first=Dan |publisher=[[CNN]] |date=20 February 2023 |access-date=1 March 2023 |archive-date=1 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230301005823/https://www.cnn.com/2023/02/19/entertainment/bafta-awards-2023-winners-list/index.html |url-status=live}} With its seven wins, ''All Quiet on the Western Front'' achieved a new record for the most BAFTAs for a film not in the English language; Show producer Nick Bullen said that criticism towards DeBose was "incredibly unfair", going on to say that she opened the show with "some energy [and] some fun". "Angela Bassett did the thing" was widely quoted by many as a stand-out quote from the rap. The true meaning of "the thing" is unknown, with [[BBC News]] saying the vagueness of the lyric contributed to its success, allowing it to be "endlessly adapted for any person and any situation". American singer and rapper [[Lizzo]] quoted the line during a performance on the European leg of her [[The Special Tour|Special Tour]], as did [[Adele]] during her "[[Weekends with Adele]]" residency.{{cite web |last=Robledo |first=Anthony |date=24 February 2023 |title=Even Lizzo Agrees That "Angela Bassett Did the Thing" |url=https://www.buzzfeednews.com/article/anthonyrobledo/lizzo-ariana-debose-angela-bassett-did-the-thing |access-date=27 February 2023 |work=[[BuzzFeed News]] |language=en |archive-date=27 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230227230154/https://www.buzzfeednews.com/article/anthonyrobledo/lizzo-ariana-debose-angela-bassett-did-the-thing |url-status=live}}{{cite web |last=Haring |first=Bruce |date=25 February 2023 |title=Ariana DeBose Gets the Last Laugh on Her BAFTAs Rap |url=https://deadline.com/2023/02/ariana-debose-gets-llast-laugh-on-baftas-rap-1235270912/ |access-date=11 April 2023 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en}} The opening number was later announced as the winner of "Best TV Musical Performance" at the 15th [[Dorian Awards|Dorian TV Awards]], given by GALECA: The Society of LGBTQ Entertainment Critics, on 26 June.{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2023/06/succession-abbott-elementary-dorian-tv-awards-winners-full-list-1235424860/ |title='Succession', 'Abbott Elementary' Top 2023 Dorian TV Awards – Winners List |last=Petski |first=Denise |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=26 June 2023 |access-date=28 June 2023}} The reception was mixed, with [[social media]] being largely responsible for the virality of the piece. Many of those name-checked in the rap praised the performance. [[Angela Bassett]] mentioned the piece during an acceptance speech at the [[54th NAACP Image Awards]] on 25 February 2023, following her win for [[NAACP Image Award for Entertainer of the Year|Entertainer of the Year]], jokingly saying: "I guess Angela Bassett did the thing!"{{cite web |last=Lindert |first=Hattie |date=27 February 2023 |title=Angela Bassett did the thing (shouted out Ariana DeBose in a speech) |url=https://www.avclub.com/angela-bassett-ariana-debose-naacp-image-awards-speech-1850162427 |access-date=27 February 2023 |work=[[The A.V. Marie, girl, what a slay': deconstructing Ariana DeBose's personalised Bafta rap |language=en-GB |work=[[The Guardian]] |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2023/feb/20/angela-bassett-did-the-thing-deconstructing-ariana-debose-bafta-performance |access-date=27 February 2023 |issn=0261-3077 |url-access=limited |archive-date=26 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230226194614/https://www.theguardian.com/film/2023/feb/20/angela-bassett-did-the-thing-deconstructing-ariana-debose-bafta-performance |url-status=live}} Conversely, Raven Smith of ''[[British Vogue]]'' praised the piece, calling it "hypnotic". ==Criticism== Despite the [[Diversity (business)|diversity]] in nominations, the winners were all white, something criticised on [[social media]] and by activist journalists, with grievances particularly directed at a photograph of all the winners on stage together, with black co-host [[Alison Hammond]] the only non-white person in it. Some [[Twitter]] users began utilising the hashtag "BaftasSoWhite", akin to the [[87th Academy Awards#Criticism regarding lack of diversity among nominees|"OscarsSoWhite" hashtag]], which [[Twitter trends|trended]] when there were no non-white performers nominated at the [[87th Academy Awards|87th]] and [[88th Academy Awards]] in 2015 and 2016, respectively. No BAFTA winner from this edition in the film acting categories, however, won at the [[95th Academy Awards|Academy Awards]] and [[29th Screen Actors Guild Awards|Screen Actors Guild Awards]] during this same year despite all of the combined winners having been nominated across the aforementioned award ceremonies.{{cite web
